title: YesBut: A High-Quality Annotated Multimodal Dataset for evaluating Satire Comprehension capability of Vision-Language Models
research_questions: Can existing Vision-Language Models decipher satire in images in zero-shot settings?
How well do SOTA VL Models perform on satirical image detection, satirical image understanding, and satirical image completion on the YesBut dataset?
Do dataset properties such as lack of text, multiple artistic styles, and disconnected sub-images make multimodal satire comprehension difficult for VL Models?
Do SOTA VL Models also struggle on real satirical photographs following the Yes, But theme?

datasets_used.name: YesBut-Satire (Nandy et al.)
datasets_used.size: 2,547 images for detection; 1,084 satirical images for understanding; 150 samples for completion

datasets_used.name: Instagram Yes, But real photo posts

methods_proposed.name: YesBut dataset construction pipeline
methods_proposed.summary: The paper collects 283 satirical Yes/But images from the X handle @_yesbut_, obtains human annotations for sub-image descriptions and overall punchlines, and augments the data with DALL-E 3-generated 2D and 3D stick-figure sub-images followed by manual satirical/non-satirical labeling.

methods_proposed.name: Satirical Image Detection task
methods_proposed.summary: Given a two-half image, the model must classify whether the combined image is satirical or non-satirical.

methods_proposed.name: Satirical Image Understanding task
methods_proposed.summary: Given a satirical image, the model generates textual descriptions of the sub-images and an explanation of why the complete image is funny or satirical.

methods_proposed.name: Satirical Image Completion task
methods_proposed.summary: Given one half of a colorized-sketch image and two candidate sub-images, the model selects the candidate that completes the Yes/But pair so that it is meaningful and satirical.

humor_theories.detail: The paper frames satire as humor that uses irony or exaggeration to criticize or mock people, politics, or society, and operationally designs images around a normal scenario plus a conflicting or ironic scenario.

evaluation_methods.detail: Satirical Image Detection is evaluated with Accuracy and F1-Score in zero-shot and zero-shot Chain-of-Thought settings.
Satirical Image Understanding is evaluated with BLEU, ROUGE-L, METEOR, BERTScore, and in the appendix the image-based Polos metric.

For Satirical Image Understanding, 30 randomly sampled images, 10 from each of Stages 2, 3, and 4, are evaluated by 3 students in the lab on binary criteria: Correctness, Appropriate Length, Visual Completeness, and Faithfulness; majority vote is taken.
Satirical Image Completion is evaluated with accuracy in zero-shot and zero-shot Chain-of-Thought settings.
For the 119 real satirical photographs, model outputs to the WHYFUNNY prompt are evaluated by a human annotator who answers whether the generated text correctly describes the satire in the image.

Five annotators annotate original satirical images with sub-image descriptions, overall punchline descriptions, text-presence features, connectedness, and annotation difficulty; a graduate student manually labels generated image combinations as satirical or non-satirical.

ChatGPT is used to classify overall descriptions into four satire types and to elaborate topic-modeling outputs into intuitive topic descriptions; it is not used to score the benchmarked VL model outputs.

human_samples.population: undergraduate sophomore students or above, enrolled in English-medium colleges
human_samples.observations: 283 satirical images annotated for left sub-image description, right sub-image description, overall punchline description, three binary image features, and annotation difficulty
human_samples.detail: These annotators performed Stage 2 annotation of the original satirical images.

human_samples.population: graduate student in the authors' lab
human_samples.observations: Manual satirical/non-satirical labeling of DALL-E 3-generated combined images; Stage 3 added 302 satirical and 547 non-satirical images, and Stage 4 added 499 satirical and 916 non-satirical images
human_samples.detail: The annotator was given 10 satirical and 10 non-satirical images prior to manual labeling as assistance.

human_samples.population: students in the lab; the paper states they were not the annotators who annotated YesBut
human_samples.observations: 30 sampled images, 10 from each of Stages 2, 3, and 4, with model-generated and human-written overall descriptions evaluated on four binary criteria; majority vote used
human_samples.detail: This human evaluation assessed Correctness, Appropriate Length, Visual Completeness, and Faithfulness.

human_samples.observations: WHYFUNNY outputs for 119 real satirical photographs evaluated for whether the generated text correctly describes the satire
human_samples.detail: The paper reports per-model understanding accuracy on the real-photograph set but does not give annotator recruitment or agreement details.

judges.detail: The authors manually downloaded and filtered 283 images from @_yesbut_ as satirical.
Five qualified student annotators wrote textual descriptions and categorical features for the 283 original satirical images.
A graduate student manually labeled generated image combinations as satirical or non-satirical.
The limitations section states that the authors manually reviewed annotations to minimize incorrect annotations.

judges.name: Accuracy and F1-Score
judges.detail: Used for Satirical Image Detection; accuracy also used for Satirical Image Completion and real-photo detection.

judges.name: BLEU, ROUGE-L, METEOR, BERTScore
judges.detail: Used for automatic evaluation of text generation in Satirical Image Understanding.

judges.name: Polos
judges.detail: Used in the appendix as an image-based metric for Satirical Understanding with the WHYFUNNY prompt.
Three lab students judged generated and human-written descriptions for the 30-image Satirical Image Understanding human evaluation subset by majority vote.
A human annotator judged whether model-generated text correctly described satire in the 119 real photographs.

judges.name: ChatGPT
judges.detail: Used to classify the original images' overall descriptions into satire types and to generate intuitive descriptions of BERTopic topics.

generation_selection.scope: DALL-E 3 synthetic sub-image generation for 2D and 3D stick-figure dataset augmentation
generation_selection.detail: The paper gives the prompts used for 2D and 3D generation and then manually labels the resulting combined images; it does not report candidate counts, reranking, or best-of-N selection.

generation_selection.scope: VL model generation for Satirical Image Understanding
generation_selection.n: 1 generated description per prompt is implied; no alternatives reported
generation_selection.detail: Models are prompted to describe sub-images and answer WHYFUNNY_PROMPT; the paper does not report generating multiple candidates or selecting the best response.

model_provenance.name: Gemini Pro Vision
model_provenance.detail: Used for all tasks; no API snapshot or inference date reported.

model_provenance.name: gpt-4-vision-preview
model_provenance.detail: Used for all tasks; no inference date reported.

model_provenance.name: LLaVA
model_provenance.detail: Open-source model run on an NVIDIA A40 GPU; exact checkpoint not reported.

model_provenance.name: MiniGPT4
model_provenance.detail: Open-source model run on an NVIDIA A40 GPU; exact checkpoint not reported.

model_provenance.name: Kosmos-2
model_provenance.detail: Open-source model run on an NVIDIA A40 GPU; exact checkpoint not reported.

model_provenance.name: DALL-E 3
model_provenance.detail: Used to generate 2D and 3D stick-figure sub-images for dataset augmentation; access route and date not reported.

model_provenance.name: ChatGPT
model_provenance.detail: Used for satire-type classification and topic description; no exact model version reported.

inference_budgets.scope: Open-source VL model inference for detection, understanding, and completion
inference_budgets.compute_or_cost: NVIDIA A40 GPU; inference time per sample goes upto around 10 seconds for Satirical Image Detection, 1 minute for Satirical Image Understanding, and 10 seconds for Satirical Image Completion
inference_budgets.detail: The paper reports compute only for open-source models and does not report decoding settings.
Zero-shot and zero-shot CoT setups are used; the non-CoT prompt constrains output to exactly Y or N. CoT prompt details and retry handling are not reported.
Models are prompted with Describe the image and WHYFUNNY_PROMPT; token limits, sampling parameters, and API cost are not reported.
Zero-shot and zero-shot CoT setups are used; the non-CoT prompt constrains output to exactly A or B.
The paper reports two prompts for generating 2D and 3D stick-figure sub-images but not number of attempts, candidate selection, cost, or generation date.

human_baselines.description: Human-written overall image descriptions are evaluated alongside model-generated descriptions. Human scores are Correctness 100.00, Appropriate Length 100.00, Visual Completeness 80.00, and Faithfulness 93.33.
human_baselines.detail: The same 30-image subset and same four binary criteria are used for human-written and model-generated descriptions.
No human accuracy baseline is reported for the detection task.
No human accuracy baseline is reported for the completion task.
The paper uses human judgment to score model understanding outputs but does not report a human task-performance baseline.

mitigations: The YesBut benchmark is newly constructed and released by the paper.
Many examples are synthetic 2D and 3D stick-figure variants generated during dataset construction.
The evaluation is zero-shot and zero-shot CoT, with no in-context exemplars.
contamination_risk.basis: The paper introduces the benchmark, but original source images come from public social media posts and real-photo posts, API model inference dates and snapshots are not reported, and no decontamination test is described.

llm_judge_dependence.affected_claims: Dataset characterization claims about the distribution of satire types and the intuitive descriptions of BERTopic topics use ChatGPT.
llm_judge_dependence.detail: The main benchmark scores are produced by automatic metrics and human evaluators, not by an LLM judge; ChatGPT is used for ancillary dataset analysis.

reporting_gaps: No exact inference dates or snapshots are reported for Gemini Pro Vision or gpt-4-vision-preview.
Exact checkpoints for LLaVA, MiniGPT4, and Kosmos-2 are not reported beyond parameter counts.
Sampling parameters, decoding settings, token budgets, retry policies, and API costs are not reported.
The zero-shot CoT prompts are not fully specified in the transcription, although the non-CoT detection and completion prompts are provided.
No inter-annotator agreement is reported for the five dataset annotators, the graduate-student generated-image labeling, or the three-person human evaluation.
Blind status for human evaluation is not reported.
Recruitment details, geography, and demographics are not reported for the human-evaluation annotators beyond lab-student status.
The real-photograph human evaluation annotator count and recruitment details are not clearly reported.
The paper does not discuss benchmark contamination or run a decontamination test.

headline_results.finding: The introduced YesBut dataset contains 2,547 images, comprising 1,084 satirical and 1,463 non-satirical images.
On Satirical Image Detection, the best test accuracy is obtained by Kosmos-2 in the zero-shot CoT setting, while the best F1 Score is obtained by Kosmos-2 in the zero-shot setting.
For Satirical Image Understanding with the WHYFUNNY prompt, Gemini gives the best Average Score at each annotation stage, but the scores remain low.
In human evaluation of Satirical Image Understanding, the aspect-wise best model remains far below human-written descriptions.
headline_results.value: Correctness 60.00 vs 100.00, Appropriate Length 56.67 vs 100.00, Visual Completeness 46.67 vs 80.00, Faithfulness 56.67 vs 93.33; gaps 40, 43.33, 33.33, 36.66 points
headline_results.best_system: GPT4 for Correctness; Gemini for Appropriate Length, Visual Completeness, and Faithfulness

headline_results.finding: On Satirical Image Completion, Gemini performs best in both zero-shot and zero-shot CoT settings.
On the 119 real satirical photographs, GPT4 obtains the highest reported detection and understanding accuracies, but all understanding accuracies are below 50%.

key_findings: No evaluated VL model exceeds 60% on both detection accuracy and F1 Score for YesBut; the best detection accuracy is 56.97 and the best F1 Score is 59.71.
Automatic understanding metrics are low: for WHYFUNNY, Gemini's best Average Scores are 0.3227, 0.321, and 0.3227 across Stages 2, 3, and 4.
The paper reports that CoT improves detection accuracy for only 2/5 models and F1 Score for only 1/5 models, suggesting limited benefit for satire detection.
For completion, CoT improves results in 3/5 models, and Gemini reaches the best zero-shot CoT accuracy of 61.81.
Human evaluation shows large gaps between model-generated and human-written satire explanations across Correctness, Appropriate Length, Visual Completeness, and Faithfulness.
On real satirical photographs, all models score below 50% on understanding accuracy, with GPT4 highest at 46.22.
limitations: The annotation task involves background knowledge that may differ among annotators, so subjectivity remains despite manual review.
The work is in English, and the authors state that they plan to extend it to languages other than English.
The human evaluation of Satirical Image Understanding uses a randomly sampled set of 30 images.
The paper does not report decontamination checks for public social-media source material.
safety_ethics_notes: The paper states that images from the X handle @_yesbut_ were downloaded with proper consent. It also notes annotation subjectivity and manual review to minimize incorrect annotations.

artifacts.other: The paper states that the dataset and code are available at the GitHub URL and additionally releases 119 real, satirical photographs for further research.
